output.summary: Fixed critical design QA issue where all tab panel summaries and backgrounds were visible on desktop. Implemented CSS-based visibility control using md:hidden and md:open:block to hide all closed details elements on desktop and only show the open/active one.

filesModified.purpose: Added md:hidden and md:open:block to hide inactive details on desktop, moved md:open:bg-accent-lighter to only show background on open details
Added md:hidden and md:open:block to hide inactive details on desktop, moved md:open:bg-accent-lighter to only show background on open details

issue.description: On desktop, all tab panels were showing their summary content and backgrounds stacked vertically. The previous revision made summary visible on desktop, but forgot that closed <details> elements still render their <summary> - they only hide content AFTER the summary.
issue.rootCause: When the architectural revision moved md:bg-accent-lighter to each <details> element and made summary visible on desktop (removed md:!hidden), all details elements became visible with their backgrounds showing. Standard browser behavior is that <details> always shows <summary> regardless of open state.
issue.impact: All tab panels were visible at once on desktop instead of only showing the active/selected tab panel

fix.approach: CSS-based visibility control using Tailwind's responsive and state variants
fix.implementation: Use md:hidden to hide all details by default on desktop, then md:open:block to show only the [open] details element

classes.before: border border-charcoal rounded-3xl md:border-0 md:bg-accent-lighter md:rounded-3xl
classes.after: border border-charcoal rounded-3xl md:hidden md:open:block md:open:bg-accent-lighter md:border-0 md:rounded-3xl

breakdown.mobile: border border-charcoal rounded-3xl - standard mobile accordion styling
breakdown.desktop_default: md:hidden - hide all details by default on desktop
breakdown.desktop_open: md:open:block md:open:bg-accent-lighter md:border-0 md:rounded-3xl - only show open details with background and rounded corners

fix.jsVerification: Verified selectTab() function properly manages details open/close states (lines 76-82 in Tabs.js). It sets 'open' attribute on active tab and removes it from all others, which is required for the CSS md:open:block selector to work.

mobile.visibility: All details elements visible, user can open/close individually
mobile.background: open:bg-accent and open:border-accent apply when user opens a details

desktop.description: Tabs interface with tablist controls and single visible panel
desktop.visibility: Only the [open] details element is visible (md:open:block), all others hidden (md:hidden)
desktop.background: Only the open details shows md:open:bg-accent-lighter background with rounded corners
desktop.control: JavaScript selectTab() manages which details has [open] attribute based on tablist button clicks

warnings: webpack performance warnings (asset size limits) - expected and not blocking
eslint exhaustive-deps warning in Tabs.tsx - expected, intentional pattern

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP have identical class structures. Both use the same responsive visibility pattern.

testPage.notes: No changes needed to test page. The fix addresses desktop rendering behavior.

fixesApplied.description: Hide inactive details elements on desktop using CSS
fixesApplied.impact: Desktop now correctly shows only the active/open tab panel instead of stacking all panels

cssExplanation.md:hidden: On desktop (md breakpoint and up), set display: none on all details elements by default
cssExplanation.md:open:block: On desktop, when details has [open] attribute, override with display: block to show it
cssExplanation.md:open:bg-accent-lighter: On desktop, only apply accent-lighter background to open details
cssExplanation.combined: The combination ensures only one details is visible on desktop (the one with [open] attribute), while mobile shows all details as normal accordions

desktopBehavior: All details elements have md:hidden applied
JavaScript selectTab() sets [open] on active tab, removes [open] from others
CSS md:open:block makes only the [open] details visible
CSS md:open:bg-accent-lighter applies background only to visible (open) details
Result: Only active tab panel visible with its background
mobileBehavior: No md: classes apply on mobile
All details visible as normal accordions
open:bg-accent applies when user opens any accordion item

notes: The fix is purely CSS-based, no JavaScript changes needed
The selectTab() function was already correctly managing [open] attributes
The md:open: variant targets details[open] only at md breakpoint and above
Mobile behavior unchanged - still works as standard details/summary accordions
Desktop now has proper tabs behavior - only one panel visible at a time
The tabColor class on each details still works correctly for both mobile and desktop
TSX and PHP are perfectly in sync
